---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: none
    width: 1050
    height: 600
    slide-number: false
---

<!--
  PROTOTYPE — single-slide anime.js version of the entanglement figure.
  One slide that transforms in place on each click (no slide change).

  LAYOUT: Me, LLM agent, .qmd, and output all sit on ONE row. The data box is
  FIXED in the lower middle and never moves; only its outgoing arrow retargets
  (-> agent in the LLM world, -> .qmd in the Quarto world). No dashed container.

  CORE IDEA: the OUTPUT is ALWAYS content + format fused. Here that's literal:
  the outer box is FORMAT (blue), the inner box is CONTENT (amber). Format is
  always fine. The variable is whether the CONTENT is trustworthy:
    - LLM world: you hand the agent your prompt AND your data; it digests both
                 probabilistically -> the content is ragged / may be wrong.
    - Quarto   : the data bypasses the agent and feeds the .qmd; render is
                 deterministic -> the content is exact (and still well formatted).

  Click sequence:
    load   : Me, LLM agent, your data.
    click 1: prompt (Me -> agent) AND data (data -> agent) arrows animate in.
    click 2: agent "generates" the output — fused, but content ragged/red.
             Review loop appears (all you can do is re-prompt).
    click 3: the .qmd box appears; agent now "writes" the .qmd; the data arrow
             swings off the agent and onto the .qmd. Output is orphaned (stale).
    click 4: render — .qmd -> output; the content snaps to clean/exact, badge
             clears, "content is exact". Still fused.
    click 5: green "you edit directly" arrows to .qmd and data. One lever -> three.

  Mechanism: empty .fragment spans are reveal's click counter; we listen to
  fragmentshown / fragmenthidden and drive anime.js to tween SVG attributes
  (Emil Hvitfeldt's tidy-animations / slidecraft "Fragments - JS" pattern).
  Color vocabulary: amber = content, blue = format/structure, green = human's
  direct action. Red is a status accent (wrong), not a concept color.
-->

```{=html}
<style>
  .figbox  { fill:#ffffff; stroke:#b9b7ae; stroke-width:1.5; }
  .figblue { fill:#E7F1FB; stroke:#3A7FC4; stroke-width:1.5; }
  .figamber{ fill:#FBEFD9; stroke:#C0820F; stroke-width:1.5; }
  .figlbl  { fill:#2b2b29; font-size:20px; font-weight:500; }
  .figsub  { fill:#6b6a65; font-size:14px; font-weight:400; }
  .figarr  { stroke:#6f6e69; stroke-width:2; fill:none; }
  .figedit { stroke:#1f8f6a; stroke-width:2.4; fill:none; }
  .figalbl { fill:#6b6a65; font-size:14px; }
  .figelbl { fill:#1f8f6a; font-size:14px; font-weight:500; }
  .figleg  { fill:#6b6a65; font-size:14px; }
  .reveal .stagesvg { width:100%; height:auto; max-height:80vh; display:block; margin:0 auto; }
  .reveal .stepdot { position:absolute; width:0; height:0; opacity:0; }
</style>
```

## Quarto keeps your content correct

```{=html}
<svg class="stagesvg" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 960 540" font-family="'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif">
<defs>
  <marker id="ah" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8.5" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto-start-reverse"><path d="M2 1.5 L8 5 L2 8.5" fill="none" stroke="context-stroke" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/></marker>
</defs>

<!-- legend -->
<g><rect class="figamber" x="50" y="36" width="16" height="16" rx="3"/><text class="figleg" x="74" y="49">content</text><rect class="figblue" x="180" y="36" width="16" height="16" rx="3"/><text class="figleg" x="204" y="49">format / structure</text></g>

<!-- actors (one row, y = 237, h = 66) -->
<g><rect class="figbox" x="50" y="237" width="130" height="66" rx="10"/><text class="figlbl" x="115" y="270" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">Me</text></g>
<g><rect class="figbox" x="250" y="237" width="160" height="66" rx="10"/><text class="figlbl" x="330" y="270" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">LLM agent</text></g>

<!-- .qmd box (Quarto world; same row, blue = format/structure) -->
<g id="qmd" style="opacity:0"><rect class="figblue" x="520" y="237" width="180" height="66" rx="10"/><text class="figlbl" x="610" y="262" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">.qmd</text><text class="figsub" x="610" y="284" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">structure + logic</text></g>

<!-- output: ALWAYS fused. outer = format (blue), inner = content (amber). -->
<g id="output" style="opacity:0">
  <rect class="figblue" x="760" y="237" width="170" height="66" rx="10"/>
  <rect id="out-content" class="figamber" x="781" y="245" width="98" height="38" rx="6" stroke="#d64a3f" stroke-dasharray="5 4"/>
  <text class="figlbl" x="845" y="227" text-anchor="middle">output</text>
  <text class="figsub" x="845" y="322" text-anchor="middle">content + format fused</text>
  <text id="cap-status" x="845" y="340" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14" font-weight="600" fill="#d64a3f">content may be wrong</text>
  <g id="out-badge"><circle cx="924" cy="232" r="12" fill="#d64a3f" stroke="#fff" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="924" y="237" text-anchor="middle" font-size="15" font-weight="700" fill="#fff">?</text></g>
</g>

<!-- data: FIXED, lower middle. Only its arrow retargets. -->
<g><rect class="figamber" x="395" y="388" width="170" height="64" rx="10"/><text class="figlbl" x="480" y="412" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">data</text><text class="figsub" x="480" y="433" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">exact content</text></g>

<!-- arrows -->
<g id="arr-prompt" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="180" y1="270" x2="246" y2="270" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="213" y="256" text-anchor="middle">prompt</text></g>
<g id="arr-data-agent" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="452" y1="388" x2="350" y2="306" marker-end="url(#ah)"/></g>
<g id="arr-generates" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="410" y1="270" x2="756" y2="270" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="583" y="256" text-anchor="middle">generates</text></g>
<g id="arr-review" style="opacity:0"><path class="figarr" d="M845 303 L845 468 L115 468 L115 303" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="480" y="458" text-anchor="middle">review / re-prompt</text></g>
<g id="arr-writes" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="410" y1="270" x2="516" y2="270" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="465" y="256" text-anchor="middle">writes</text></g>
<g id="arr-reads" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="530" y1="388" x2="602" y2="306" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="588" y="356" text-anchor="start">reads</text></g>
<g id="arr-render" style="opacity:0"><line class="figarr" x1="700" y1="270" x2="756" y2="270" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figalbl" x="728" y="256" text-anchor="middle">render</text></g>
<g id="arr-edits" style="opacity:0"><path class="figedit" d="M185 248 Q 360 150 516 250" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><path class="figedit" d="M125 300 Q 250 432 392 418" marker-end="url(#ah)"/><text class="figelbl" x="320" y="150" text-anchor="middle">you edit directly</text></g>
</svg>
```

<span class="stepdot fragment" data-step="1"></span>
<span class="stepdot fragment" data-step="2"></span>
<span class="stepdot fragment" data-step="3"></span>
<span class="stepdot fragment" data-step="4"></span>
<span class="stepdot fragment" data-step="5"></span>

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/animejs@3.2.1/lib/anime.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  var DUR = 700;

  function render(step, animate) {
    if (!window.anime) return;
    var d = animate ? DUR : 0, e = 'easeInOutCubic';

    anime({ targets:'#arr-prompt',     opacity: step>=1?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-data-agent', opacity: (step>=1 && step<=2)?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-generates',  opacity: step==2?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#output',         opacity: step>=2?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-review',     opacity: step>=2?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#qmd',            opacity: step>=3?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-writes',     opacity: step>=3?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-reads',      opacity: step>=3?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-render',     opacity: step>=4?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#out-badge',      opacity: (step>=2 && step<4)?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });
    anime({ targets:'#arr-edits',      opacity: step>=5?1:0, duration:d, easing:e });

    // the payoff: content snaps from ragged/red -> exact/amber, filling the format box
    var correct = step>=4;
    anime({ targets:'#out-content',
            x:      correct ? 792 : 781,
            y:      correct ? 251 : 245,
            width:  correct ? 106 : 98,
            stroke: correct ? '#C0820F' : '#d64a3f',
            duration:d, easing:e });
    var c = document.getElementById('out-content');
    if (c) c.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', correct ? '' : '5 4');

    var st = document.getElementById('cap-status');
    if (st) {
      st.textContent = correct ? 'content is exact' : 'content may be wrong';
      st.setAttribute('fill', correct ? '#2b2b29' : '#d64a3f');
    }
  }

  function setup() {
    if (!window.Reveal) { return setTimeout(setup, 50); }
    var go = function (n) { render(n, true); };
    Reveal.on('fragmentshown',  function (e) {
      if (e.fragment && e.fragment.dataset.step) go(+e.fragment.dataset.step);
    });
    Reveal.on('fragmenthidden', function (e) {
      if (e.fragment && e.fragment.dataset.step) go(+e.fragment.dataset.step - 1);
    });
    Reveal.on('ready', function () { render(0, false); });
    if (Reveal.isReady && Reveal.isReady()) render(0, false);
  }
  setup();
})();
</script>
```

::: {.notes}
Load: you, the agent, and your data. Click 1: you hand the agent a prompt AND your data. Click 2: it generates the output — format's fine, but the content is ragged and unverified; all you can do is review and re-prompt. Click 3: the .qmd appears; now the agent only writes the .qmd, and your data arrow swings off the agent and onto the .qmd — your content never passes through the model. Click 4: the .qmd renders to the output; the content snaps to exact, still perfectly formatted. Click 5: you edit structure and content directly. One lever became three.
:::
